fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Nebraska Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Nebraska - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity nebraska, how to transfer audiologist license to nebraska, audiologist compact nebraska, nebraska audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology
board.parent_dept: Nebraska Department of Health and Human Services (DHHS)
board.mailing_address:
Nebraska Department of Health and Human Services
Credentialing Division
P.O. Box 94986
Lincoln, NE 68509-4986

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years (by September 1 of odd-numbered years)

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Nebraska.

steps.title: Create NECO Account and Start Application
steps.description: Register and log in to the Nebraska Credentialing Online (NECO) system at https://ne.gov/go/NECO and initiate the Audiologist license application.

steps.title: Submit Application and Pay Fee
steps.description: Complete the online application form within NECO and pay the non-refundable $110.00 application fee.

steps.title: Arrange for Official Transcripts
steps.description: Request official transcripts from all post-secondary institutions where you completed audiology coursework to be sent directly to the Nebraska DHHS Credentialing Division.

steps.title: Submit Verification of Clinical Experience
steps.description: Have your supervising audiologist complete and submit the official Verification of Clinical Experience (VCE) form directly to the Nebraska DHHS Credentialing Division.

steps.title: Provide National Exam Scores and Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Arrange for official verification of your passing score on the national audiology examination (e.g., Praxis or ASHA CCC-A) to be sent, and complete the required fingerprint-based criminal background check through DHHS-approved channels.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Nebraska audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Nebraska have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Nebraska participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Nebraska without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Nebraska?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Nebraska while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in Nebraska
hero.dek: Audiologist licensing in Nebraska is managed through the Nebraska Credentialing Online (NECO) system, where all applications must be submitted.

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.registration_steps: To apply for an Audiologist license in Nebraska, create an account on the Nebraska Credentialing Online (NECO) system at https://ne.gov/go/NECO. Click 'Register' for a new user account, then log in to select 'Apply for a new license' and choose 'Audiologist'.
